channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Not known for political coverage, Wired takes a leading role in tracking Elon Musk's team
item.description: The tech outlet Wired set up a new politics team in time for the presidential election — but its most impactful work is coming after the election is over.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Satya Nadella says AI is yet to have its Excel moment
item.description: Microsoft CEO thinks AI should be measured in its contribution to an economy, rather than benchmarks of intelligence.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of president
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday evening shared a video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) that showcases his proposals for the potential development of the war-torn Gaza Strip, complete with a golden statue of his likeness and beachfront skyscrapers rising from the rubble.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Warner Bros. shutters 3 video-game studios, 2 in California
item.description: The cuts reflect a refocusing of the games division on major franchises such as Harry Potter, Mortal Kombat, Game of Thrones and DC Comics, primarily Batman.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cómo pueden los CIO ayudar a que el futuro sea menos desagradable que el presente
item.description: Los CIO que quieran prosperar en el futuro deben escapar de un presente poco celebrado. La tecnología en general ha perdido popularidad. La ciencia ficción ha sido invadida por la distopía y los escenarios apocalípticos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Riyadh Air and IBM forge AI-Driven partnership to revolutionize aviation
item.description: In a groundbreaking move, Riyadh Air has announced a strategic partnership with IBM to integrate AI across its operations, aiming to establish itself as the world’s first digital-native airline.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI, automation spur efforts to upskill network pros
item.description: Networking skills are evolving to keep pace with broader technology trends, and network pros need to proactively develop new competencies in areas such as AI and automation to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: INE Secures Spot in G2’s 2025 Top 50 Education Software Rankings
item.description: INE, the leading provider of networking and cybersecurity training and certifications, today announced its recognition as an enterprise and small business leader in online course providers and cybersecurity professional development, along with its designation as the recipient of G2’s 2025 Best Softw
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE is draining the life from the once-vaunted U.S. Digital Service
item.description: The United States Digital Service (USDS), the storied group of Silicon Valley types brought together by Obama to bring government services into the 21st century, will likely never be the same after the arrival of Elon Musk’s so-called Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Three tropical cyclones are swirling in the South Pacific
item.description: Three tropical cyclones are spinning in the South Pacific, an occurrence that scientists say is unusual.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use Gemini AI to write (and rewrite) in Google Docs and Gmail
item.description: Whether you’re a professional writer or someone who dreads having to compose documents or emails for your job, the potential of AI assistance for your writing tasks is appealing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 strategies for carbon-free data centers
item.description: The construction of massive data center campuses is booming, with hyperscalers, colocation providers and large enterprises developing new capacity to support the exploding requirements of AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: MongoDB acquires Voyage AI to reduce hallucinations in AI applications
item.description: Database engine developer MongoDB has acquired Voyage AI in order to help enterprises reduce hallucinations in AI-powered applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel targets edge, high-performance computing with extended Xeon 6 chip line
item.description: Intel has expanded its Xeon 6 line of processors, adding models 6700/6500 for high-performance cores and edge computing devices to the family. The new Xeon 6 processors feature Performance-cores (P-cores) for high performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This is what they never told you about online privacy
item.description: Understanding the language of the internet can help you protect your personal information from bad actors. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation
item.description: Leo Garciga, the Army’s CIO, said the Unified Network Optimization initiative is helping the service cut costs and duplicative applications. The post Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
